title: Four-Variable Karnaugh Map (K-map)
description: A visual representation of a 4-variable (A, B, C, D) Karnaugh Map used to simplify Boolean algebra expressions by grouping adjacent '1' cells.
elements: Cell Values (0 or 1 representing the output of a minterm)
Groupings (loops encircling adjacent '1's in powers of two: 1,2,4,8,16)
relationships: The grid rows are labeled with the Gray code sequence for variables A and B
The grid columns are labeled with the Gray code sequence for variables C and D
Each cell represents a unique combination (minterm) of the four input variables
Adjacent cells (including wrap-around edges) differ by only one variable, allowing them to be grouped to eliminate that variable
